name: Cowpoke Chili
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Dry pinto beans or dry navy
12 Canned serrano peppers, seeded and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the rind from the bacon and cut into half-inch squares.
2. Cut the bacon into small pieces; set aside.
3. Add the rind, beans, onion, garlic, and Ancho peppers to a large heavy kettle.
4. Pour in water and bring to a boil.
5. Lower the flame, cover the kettle, and let the beans cook gently for about 1½ hours.
6. Stir in salt and continue cooking uncovered for 15 minutes.
7. In a separate pan, fry the diced bacon until slightly crisp.
8. Add diced tomatoes and remaining ingredients (serrano peppers, green hot pepper) to the bacon.
9. Cook this mixture over medium flame for about 10 minutes.
10. Skim off any excess fat.
11. Stir in the bacon and tomato mixture into the beans.
12. Continue cooking uncovered over a low flame for another hour or until the beans are very tender.
Makes about 3 quarts.
